response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile generalist engineer with deep experience in TDD, high-performance systems, and full-stack web work, but the role demands a C++ specialist (C++/STL/OOP, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT) within industrial automation under IEC 62443 secure-development standards. C++ appears only in dated, minor stints (LabWindows/CVI C/C++ components, a bachelor thesis) and is absent from the listed skills; the core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of message-broker architectures, MQTT/industrial protocols, IEC 62443/SDL security practice, or industrial automation domain experience, so the match to this specific role is weak.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience with strong craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Proven high-performance, multi-threaded, low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, microservices at scale)
Demonstrated mentoring and communication via workshops, talks, and courses
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and dated; not even listed among current skills
No IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, message brokers, or industrial automation protocol experience
Cloud exposure is AWS, not the advantageous Azure; primary stack is web/Node, not the required C++
